mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 2.5% annually signals central bank tightening, which increases equity discount rates and reduces forward S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5%, or the correlation between CPIAUCSL and SPX returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in industrial production (INDPRO) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates robust economic demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns through improved corporate earnings expectations over 60 days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between INDPRO and HOUST fails to explain > 15% of SPX return variance in out-of-sample tests, or the combined effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A tightening labor market (UNRATE < 5%) correlates with rising wage pressures that boost consumer spending, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in > 40% of periods where UNRATE falls below 5%, or the predictive power of UNRATE is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production (INDPRO) signals weakening supply-side capacity, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as earnings expectations decline.
failure_condition: SPX returns are positive in > 50% of instances where INDPRO declines by 1.5%+ m/m, or the directional relationship breaks down (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP (GDPC1) and retail sales (RSAFS) reflects strong aggregate demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through improved corporate profit outlooks.
failure_condition: The joint effect of GDPC1 and RSAFS on SPX returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.05), or the interaction term explains < 10% of return variance.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS > 200k) indicates labor market strength, which correlates with positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days through elevated consumer confidence.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in > 35% of periods with PAYEMS > 200k, or the relationship lacks statistical significance (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS > 4.5%) signals monetary tightening, which increases equity risk premiums and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are positive in > 50% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or the directional relationship is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).
